label: Transparent flying fish fins motif
prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: Morphous Flying Fish transparent motif chroma-key source
Primary request: Create only a flying fish for a design-system catalog, isolated as a single complete subject on a removable chroma-key background.
Subject: an accurate oceanic flying fish in graceful gliding profile, full body visible, long pectoral fins spread like wings, streamlined silver-blue body, pearlescent belly, translucent blue-silver wing fins with fine ray structure, small dark eye, forked tail, subtle open-ocean sheen. The identity must read as flying fish, not generic sardine, mackerel, blue tang, or butterflyfish.
Composition/framing: centered complete cutout with generous padding; include the entire fish, both wing fins, nose, tail, and silhouette without cropping. Face points right. Clean studio light preserving silver, blue, translucent fin detail, and oceanic highlights. No cast shadow.
Transparent workflow: place the fish on a perfectly flat solid #ff00ff chroma-key background for background removal. The background must be one uniform color with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, lighting variation, or texture. Do not use #ff00ff anywhere in the fish.
Constraints: no ocean scene, no waves, no water splash, no bubbles, no props, no second fish, no text, no watermark, no logo, no frame.

label: Light-mode design-system board
prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous Flying Fish light-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive light-mode design-system board for a Morphous theme named "Morphous Flying Fish", derived from the visible flying fish motif: silver-blue streamlined body, translucent wing-like pectoral fins, fine fin rays, pearlescent belly, swift open-ocean travel mood. This board is a source reference for implementing a shadcn/tweakcn-compatible swift travel system.
Canvas: exact wide 16:9 design-system presentation, intended final asset 3840 x 2160. Make it feel like a polished 4K product design board with large readable labels, precise grid, and no tiny text.
Required visible sections: Morphous Flying Fish, Flying Fish Fins, Palette, Typography, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Travel Dashboard / Data, Forms, Table, Command / Search, Status States, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadow, Tokens, Generated Asset Examples.
Reference use: Extract colors and interaction language from a flying fish: silver-blue body, translucent fin blue, pearl white belly, deep open-ocean navy, mist cyan edge highlights, graphite fin rays, sea-glass aqua, pale foam surfaces. The board must read as flying fish fins and swift open-ocean travel, not a generic blue UI kit.
Typography: English travel and operations UI examples with Japanese typography guidance; use short readable labels such as Geist, Display, Body, Label, Numbers, Noto Sans JP, Japanese UI, headings, body, labels, numbers. Use tabular numbers for route times and dashboard metrics.
UI coverage: primary/secondary/ghost/destructive buttons, hover/pressed/disabled/loading states, icon buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, segmented control, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, metric cards, travel navigation sidebar, top nav, breadcrumb, table rows, data filters, command search palette, date picker, pagination, empty/loading/error/success states, progress, stepper, tooltip, dropdown menu, line chart, bar chart, donut chart, area chart, spacing scale, radius samples, border tokens, shadow tokens, texture samples, small travel icon set, token swatches.
Charts: include visible line, bar, donut, and area charts using silver blue, translucent fin blue, sea-glass aqua, pearl white, and deep ocean navy.
Style: crisp shadcn/tweakcn product UI, restrained 8px card radius, aerodynamic horizontal layouts, fin-ray dividers, translucent glassy overlays, clean route-planning panels, fast scanning dashboards, pale foam background, pearl surface cards, open-ocean navy text, silver-blue primary actions, cyan focus rings, graphite separators, subtle wave-shear and fin-ray texture samples.
Color palette: silver blue, translucent fin blue, sea-glass aqua, mist cyan, pearl white, open-ocean navy, graphite, pale foam. Avoid unrelated colors.
Constraints: no generic purple or blue gradient dominance, no neon nightclub look, no dark-only board, no stock ocean photo collage, no extra animals, no random brand names, no watermark, no misspelling of "Morphous Flying Fish", no tiny unreadable paragraphs.

label: Dark-mode design-system board
prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous Flying Fish dark-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive dark-mode design-system board for the Morphous theme named "Morphous Flying Fish" / "Flying Fish Fins". Translate the same visible flying fish motif and light design-system board into dark mode; do not invent a new unrelated theme. It remains a swift open-ocean travel system optimized for dark-mode route, fleet, and operations dashboards.
Canvas: exact wide 16:9 design-system presentation, intended final asset 3840 x 2160. Make it feel like a polished 4K product design board with large readable labels, precise grid, and no tiny text.
Required visible sections: Morphous Flying Fish, Flying Fish Fins, Dark Mode, Palette, Typography, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Travel Dashboard / Data, Forms, Table, Command / Search, Status States, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadow, Tokens, Generated Asset Examples.
Reference use: Preserve the same motif-derived language: silver-blue body, translucent fin blue, pearl white belly, deep open-ocean navy, mist cyan edge highlights, graphite fin rays, sea-glass aqua, fin-ray dividers, wave-shear texture, and crisp shadcn/tweakcn component language. The board must read as flying fish fins and swift open-ocean travel, not generic dark blue UI.
Typography: English travel and operations UI examples with Japanese typography guidance; use short readable labels such as Geist, Display, Body, Label, Numbers, Noto Sans JP, Japanese UI, headings, body, labels, numbers. Use tabular numbers for route times and dashboard metrics.
UI coverage: primary/secondary/ghost/destructive buttons, hover/pressed/disabled/loading states, icon buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, segmented control, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, metric cards, travel navigation sidebar, top nav, breadcrumb, table rows, data filters, command search palette, date picker, pagination, empty/loading/error/success states, progress, stepper, tooltip, dropdown menu, line chart, bar chart, donut chart, area chart, spacing scale, radius samples, border tokens, shadow tokens, texture samples, small travel icon set, token swatches.
Charts: include visible line, bar, donut, and area charts using fin blue, sea-glass aqua, mist cyan, pearl white, graphite, and deep ocean navy.
Visual details: deep ocean navy background, blue-black panels, translucent fin-blue active states, mist-cyan focus rings, pearl text, graphite separators, silver-blue chart strokes, sea-glass success chips, coral-red destructive states kept minimal, subtle fin-ray and wave-shear texture overlays, clean route-planning cards, 8px radius, crisp controls and dashboards.
Color palette: deep ocean navy, blue-black, silver blue, translucent fin blue, sea-glass aqua, mist cyan, pearl white, graphite. Avoid unrelated colors.
Constraints: no neon nightclub look, no pure black flat background, no generic purple or blue gradient dominance, no gaming UI, no stock ocean photo collage, no extra animals, no random brand names, no watermark, no misspelling of "Morphous Flying Fish", no tiny unreadable paragraphs.
